stimulus: In 1855, a Cherokee surveyor named Sequoyah Tahlonteeskee drew a finely detailed map of the Arkansas River basin. This work demonstrates a great deal of geographic knowledge, as the map features precise names of tributaries and ridges, as well as the first known sketch of the basin's seasonal floodplain. The map is especially notable because Tahlonteeskee also marked the locations of several Indigenous communities living in the region.
stem: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?

answerOptions.content: It explains how Tahlonteeskee developed an interest in surveying.
It details how the map was used for trade and travel.
It emphasizes Tahlonteeskee's wish to represent communities on the map.
It identifies some reasons why the map is impressive.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The underlined sentence highlights features—precise names, the first known sketch of the floodplain—that make the map impressive.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence doesn't describe how Tahlonteeskee became a surveyor. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence doesn't describe how the map was used. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence doesn't focus on Indigenous communities—that's mentioned afterward.
